title: HTTP Client Reuse, Retrieval, Redirects, Validators, and Bounded Bodies · Language Clinic: Bounded Streaming
summary: Each page creates a new client, sends browser-like lies, follows redirects outside scope, reads unlimited bodies, and accepts an HTML-looking error page. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the bounded streaming increment in bounded HTTP page-fetch contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case prac…
objectives: Enforce compressed and expanded byte limits during streaming, close partial bodies, and retain a visible rejection reason.
Resolve each redirect, reapply authorization, origin, credential, method, loop, and budget policy, and retain the complete chain.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-http-fetch-contract-3
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-http-fetch-contract-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance.
def worked_scraper_http_fetch_contract_3(responses: int = 12, admitted_html: int = 8, rejected_bodies: int = 4, redirect_hops: int = 3, leaked_clients: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorizationworked3",
        "url5798",
        "response3dekrow",
        "treeworked35798",
        "frontier5798worked3",
        "report3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if responses < 1 or admitted_html < 0 or rejected_bodies < 0:
        return False, responses, "response-count"
    if admitted_html + rejected_bodies != responses:
        return False, admitted_html, "admission-reconciliation"
    if redirect_hops < 0 or redirect_hops > 5:
        return False, redirect_hops, "redirect-budget"
    if leaked_clients > 0:
        return False, leaked_clients, "client-cleanup"
    assert admitted_html + rejected_bodies == responses and leaked_clients == 0
    return True, admitted_html, "bounded-page-fetch"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Build Bounded Streaming evidence · Theory Bounded Streaming
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-bounded-streaming-1y9wkf" that uses case 3814 to validate client ownership, response admission, redirect revalidation, bounded streaming, and cleanup. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Build Bounded Streaming evidence · Theory Bounded Streaming.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Bounded Streaming into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Build Bounded Streaming evidence · Theory Bounded Streaming, keep Bounded Streaming connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-bounded-streaming-1y9wkf
# Competency: scraper-bounded-streaming.
# Case 3814: an authorized nonprofit content audit.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def evidence_scraper_bounded_streaming_1y9wkf(responses: int = 12, admitted_html: int = 8, rejected_bodies: int = 4, redirect_hops: int = 3, leaked_clients: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1y9wkf = "1y9wkf-fkw9y1-3814"
    assert evidence_variant_1y9wkf
    evidence_axes_1y9wkf = (
        "authorization1y9wkf",
        "url3814",
        "responsefkw9y1",
        "tree1y9wkf3814",
        "frontier38141y9wkf",
        "reportfkw9y13814",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1y9wkf)) == 6
    if responses < 1 or admitted_html < 0 or rejected_bodies < 0:
        return False, responses, "response-count"
    if admitted_html + rejected_bodies != responses:
        return False, admitted_html, "admission-reconciliation"
    if redirect_hops < 0 or redirect_hops > 5:
        return False, redirect_hops, "redirect-budget"
    if leaked_clients > 0:
        return False, leaked_clients, "client-cleanup"
    assert admitted_html + rejected_bodies == responses and leaked_clients == 0
    return True, admitted_html, "bounded-page-fetch"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Enforce compressed and expanded byte limits during streaming, close partial bodies, and retain a visible rejection reason.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: scraper-bounded-streaming-1y9wkf(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_scraper_bounded_streaming_1y9wkf\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?responses < 1 or admitted_html < 0 or rejected_bodies < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?admitted_html \+ rejected_bodies != responses)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?redirect_hops < 0 or redirect_hops > 5)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?leaked_clients > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
